body_txt: In the media, New Jersey is often portrayed one of three ways: There is also an additional quasi-trope, which might involve any of these flavors or none of them, particularly prominent among media written by New Yorkers: the Shore as a setting, typically as a destination for white out-of-state tourists with money (and usually more of that than sense). A beach house may pop up. Ironically: despite the state being a well-known butt of jokes in American popular culture, a surprisingly large number of iconic and beloved fictional characters are canonically from New Jersey. While it's fairly well-known that Tony Soprano and Jay and Silent Bob are New Jerseyans, not as many people know that Indiana Jones , Jason Voorhees , and Batman were born there too. note Yes, you read that last one right: numerous official sources from DC Comics (dating back to at least 1977) have officially stated that Gotham City is in New Jersey. At least one incarnation of The Addams Family has also depicted the Addamses as residing in the state, likely as a nod to Charles Addams being from Westfield. Not to be confused with Funetik Aksent . Oh, and:  For information on the various regions, cities and locales of the state of New Jersey, as well as a long list of famous New Jerseyans, check out our handy Useful Notes page . Not to be confused with that other Jersey , after which this one was named.
